# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*'

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write # To create GitHub releases
      id-token: write # For OIDC authentication to PyPI (if used)
      packages: write # If publishing to GitHub Packages

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # --- Application Build Steps (Customize these) ---
      - name: Setup Node.js (for npm packages)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18' # Or your desired version
          cache: 'npm' # Or yarn, pnpm
      - name: Install Node.js dependencies
        run: npm ci # Or yarn install --frozen-lockfile, pnpm install

      - name: Setup Python (for PyPI packages)
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.x' # Or your desired version
          cache: 'pip'
      - name: Install Python dependencies
        run: pip install poetry && poetry install # Example with Poetry

      - name: Build application/packages
        run: |
          echo "Building Node.js package..."
          npm run build # Replace with your actual build command
          echo "Node.js package built."
          echo "Building Python package..."
          poetry build # Replace with your actual Python build command
          echo "Python package built."
        # Add actual build commands for your project
        # For example, for a Node.js project:
        # run: npm run build
        # For a Python project:
        # run: python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel

      # --- Create GitHub Release ---
      - name: Get tag name
        id: get_tag
        run: echo "TAG_NAME=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          name: Release ${{ steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG_NAME }}
          tag_name: ${{ steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG_NAME }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
          # You can attach artifacts here, e.g.:
          # files: |
          #   ./dist/*.zip
          #   ./build/*.tar.gz
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # This token is automatically provided

      # --- Publish to npm ---
      - name: Publish to npm
        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') # Ensure it's a version tag
        uses: JS-DevTools/npm-publish@v3
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }} # You need to set NPM_TOKEN in your repository secrets
          # access: public # Or restricted
          # cwd: ./dist # If your package.json is in a subdirectory
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }} # Required for some npm clients

      # --- Publish to PyPI ---
      - name: Publish to PyPI
        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') # Ensure it's a version tag
        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
        with:
          user: __token__
          password: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }} # You need to set PYPI_API_TOKEN in your repository secrets
          # repository_url: https://test.pypi.org/legacy/ # For TestPyPI
          # You might need to adjust the path to your distribution files, e.g.:
          # path: dist/*
          # Or if using trusted publishers (recommended):
          # trusted_publisher: true # Ensure your project is configured on PyPI for trusted publishers
